section_policy.guidance: After the title, overview, and table of contents, choose feature-specific topic sections. Do not use a fixed section template for every feature. Sections should explain meaningful usage themes rather than list APIs mechanically.

rules: Use dali-ui symbols as the app-facing API surface.
Use dali-core and dali-adaptor symbols only as setup/background unless the usage pattern explicitly marks them preferred.
Prefer View-based examples over raw Actor object-model examples.

usage_patterns.intent: Make a View respond to input through the interactive trait.
usage_patterns.usage_note: Obtain the trait from View, then connect signals on the trait. Do not pass trait objects to unrelated effect APIs.

usage_patterns.intent: Animate a View using the dali-ui app-facing animation API.
usage_patterns.usage_note: Use View as the object being animated. Inherited transform property enums are owned by Dali::Actor::Property.

symbols.brief: Actor is the primary object with which Dali applications interact.

members.brief: Creates an initialized Actor.
members.name: New

members.brief: Sets a flag to identify whether the Actor is ignored or not. If the actor is marked as ignored, it will not be rendered and will be excluded from render thread computation. So, the current properties like WorldPosition and WorldColor become inaccurate.
members.name: SetIgnored

members.brief: Sets the resize policy to be used for the given dimension(s).
members.name: SetResizePolicy

members.brief: Get total number of cache renderers.
members.name: GetCacheRendererCount

members.brief: Retrieve and child actor by index.
members.name: GetChildAt

members.brief: Retrieves the number of children held by the actor.
members.name: GetChildCount

members.brief: Calculates the height of the actor given a width.
members.name: GetHeightForWidth

members.brief: Gets the layer in which the actor is present.
members.name: GetLayer

members.brief: Returns the natural size of the actor.
members.name: GetNaturalSize

members.brief: Retrieves the actor's parent.
members.name: GetParent

members.brief: Returns the value of negotiated dimension for the given dimension.
members.name: GetRelayoutSize

members.brief: Gets a Renderer by index.
members.name: GetRendererAt

members.brief: Gets the number of renderers on this actor.
members.name: GetRendererCount

members.brief: Returns the resize policy used for a single dimension.
members.name: GetResizePolicy

members.brief: Retrieves the actor's size.
members.name: GetTargetSize

members.brief: Calculates the width of the actor given a height.
members.name: GetWidthForHeight

members.brief: This signal is emitted when hover input is received.
members.name: HoveredSignal

members.brief: This signal is emitted when the visible property of this actor or any of its parents (right up to the root layer) changes.
members.name: InheritedVisibilityChangedSignal

members.brief: This signal is emitted when the layout direction property of this or a parent actor is changed.
members.name: LayoutDirectionChangedSignal

members.brief: This signal is emitted after the actor has been disconnected from the scene.
members.name: OffSceneSignal

members.brief: This signal is emitted after the size has been set on the actor during relayout.
members.name: OnRelayoutSignal

members.brief: This signal is emitted after the actor has been connected to the scene.
members.name: OnSceneSignal

members.brief: This signal is emitted when touch input is received.
members.name: TouchedSignal

members.brief: This signal is emitted when wheel event is received.
members.name: WheelEventSignal

members.brief: Returns whether the Actor is ignored or not.
members.name: IsIgnored

members.brief: Copy constructor.
members.name: Actor

members.brief: Move constructor.
members.name: Actor

members.brief: Creates an uninitialized Actor; this can be initialized with Actor::New().
members.name: Actor

members.brief: Adds a child Actor to this Actor.
members.name: Add

members.brief: Add renderer drawing cached output to this Actor.
members.name: AddCacheRenderer

members.brief: Adds a renderer to this actor.
members.name: AddRenderer

members.brief: Downcasts a handle to Actor handle.
members.name: DownCast

members.brief: Search through this actor's hierarchy for an actor with the given unique ID.
members.name: FindChildById

members.brief: Search through this actor's hierarchy for an actor with the given name.
members.name: FindChildByName

members.brief: Lower the actor below the previous sibling actor.
members.name: Lower

members.brief: Lower the actor to below the target actor.
members.name: LowerBelow

members.brief: Lower actor to the bottom of all other sibling actors.
members.name: LowerToBottom

members.brief: Raise actor above the next sibling actor.
members.name: Raise

members.brief: Raises the actor above the target actor.
members.name: RaiseAbove

members.brief: Raise actor above all other sibling actors.
members.name: RaiseToTop

The flag whether a child actor inherits it's parent's orientation.
The flag whether a child actor inherits it's parent's position.
The flag whether a child actor inherits it's parent's scale.
The flag whether the actor is of class Dali::Layer.
The flag whether an actor is the root actor, which is owned by the Scene.
The flag whether the actor should be focusable by keyboard navigation.
The direction of the layout.
The flag whether an actor should receive a notification when touch or hover motion events leave. In the case of the hover event, when the hover event enters the actor, it will receive started state.
The maximum size an actor can be assigned in size negotiation.
The minimum size an actor can be assigned in size negotiation.
The name of an actor.
The opacity of the actor.
The orientation of an actor.
The padding of an actor for use in layout.
The origin of an actor, within its parent's area.

The y origin of an actor, within its parent's area.
The z origin of an actor, within its parent's area.

The y position of an actor.
The z position of an actor.
The scale factor applied to an actor.
The x scale factor applied to an actor.

The x scale factor applied to an actor.

The flag whether an actor should emit touch or hover signals.

The flag to determine the width dependent on the height.
The resize policy for the width of an actor.
The world color of an actor.
The world matrix of an actor.

The z world position of an actor.
The world scale factor applied to an actor.
Raise actor above the next sibling actor.
Raises the actor above the target actor.
Raise actor above all other sibling actors.
Removes a child Actor from this Actor.
Removes cache renderer from the Actor.
Removes a renderer from the actor.
Removes a renderer from the actor by index.
Applies a relative rotation to an actor.
Applies a relative rotation to an actor.
Applies a relative rotation to an actor.
Applies a relative scale to an actor.
Converts screen coordinates into the actor's coordinate system using the default camera.

Sets a flag to identify whether the Actor is ignored or not. If the actor is marked as ignored, it will not be rendered and will be excluded from render thread computation. So, the current properties like WorldPosition and WorldColor become inaccurate.
Interface for interaction traits attached to a View.

rules: Use dali-ui symbols as the app-facing API surface.
Use dali-core and dali-adaptor symbols only as setup/background unless the usage pattern explicitly marks them preferred.
Prefer View-based examples over raw Actor object-model examples.

usage_patterns.intent: Make a View respond to input through the interactive trait.
usage_patterns.usage_note: Obtain the trait from View, then connect signals on the trait. Do not pass trait objects to unrelated effect APIs.

usage_patterns.intent: Animate a View using the dali-ui app-facing animation API.
usage_patterns.usage_note: Use View as the object being animated. Inherited transform property enums are owned by Dali::Actor::Property.
